title: Trade-in Circularity Rate
bodyHtml:
The circularity rate is a Trade-in key performance indicator (KPI) that measures the percentage of products sold on Back Market that were sourced through Back Market Trade-in.

We measure this to ensure the Trade-in program is providing you with additional supply to boost your sales but also to ensure circularity within Back Market.

 

This KPI is one of the factors used in the Trade-in BackBox algorithm to help you maximize this virtuous circle.

 

Learn more about Trade-in BackBox.

 

How is it calculated?

This rate is recalculated every day to show how many Trade-in products you've bought and then sold on Back Market over the past 6 weeks. 
Basically, we count all the Trade-in devices you've paid for and then, we check how many of those devices you've sold on Back Market. As Back Market is not collecting unique identifiers for all products (e.g. IMEI for smartphones), the comparison is made based on the Back Market product ID. 

Note that:

 

  • This KPI includes a 1-week lag between the trade-in payment date and the selling date to reflect the average time to refurbish and sell back a device you sourced.
  • Non-functional devices that you source account only for 0.5 of a device. This means that sourcing 2 of such devices and reselling one would still get you a 100% circularity rate for these devices. The example below shows the calculation in action!

 

Example: Seller A sourced and paid for 100 iPhones 13, 128 GB between 1 April and 6 May. They sold 70 iPhone 13, 128 GB between 8 April and 13 May. Therefore, on 13 May, the Circularity Rate of Seller A for this product is 70%.

 

How to track your circularity rate?

You will be able to track your circularity rate from your Back Office shortly, by checking your Trade-in insights (Sales > Insights > Trade-in).

 

In the meantime, we invite you to track the products that you sell back on Back Market to improve your BackBox price.

 

How to improve your circularity rate?

Improving your trade-in circularity rate is fairly simple, you need to refurbish and resell the devices you sourced through Trade-in on Back Market.

 

We suggest you source devices with the best margin opportunities.
